The director has five scenes inspired by Akshay Kumar's movies released in 1994-2015-2012-2004-2007. The scenes require 4-12-10-6-8 days respectively. The release years fall into three decades: 1990-1999, 2000-2009, and 2010-2019. The scenes from 1994-2004-2007 belong to the 1990-1999 and 2000-2009 decades, while 2012 and 2015 belong to the 2010-2019 decade. The constraint is that no two consecutive scenes can be from the same decade. The optimal shooting order is 1994-2004-2012-2007-2015. This sequence alternates between decades and minimizes the total number of shooting days by 4+6+10+8+12=40 days. The total shooting days are minimized to 40 days while adhering to the decade constraint.
******
The director has a budget of 50 lakhs and five scenes with daily costs of 1-1.5-2-2.5-3 lakhs. The goal is to maximize the number of scenes completed within the budget. Let x1-x5 be the number of days each scene is shot. The constraints are 1x1+1.5x2+2x3+2.5x4+3x5<=50, and x1,x2,x3,x4,x5>=0. The objective is to maximize the number of scenes completed. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is 4. One possible solution is to shoot scenes with 4-6-8-10 days. Another possible solution is to shoot scenes with 4-6-8-12 days. Both combinations stay within the 50 lakh budget. The maximum number of scenes is 4.
